location_cue: Fremont's eastern hills and canyon areas
resident_meaning: Fremont's annex describes extensive East Bay Hills open space and applies wildland-urban-interface fire requirements in designated high fire hazard areas. An address in the eastern hills may therefore need earlier evacuation and route planning, but the LHMP text is not a parcel fire-zone determination.
before_actions: Turn on official alerts and identify two ways to leave the hills or canyon area.
Plan for smoke, power interruption, pets, medications, and transportation before fire-weather conditions.
during_actions: Follow official evacuation instructions and leave early if routes may become constrained.
after_actions: Wait for official re-entry information and document evacuation, smoke, and property impacts.
recovery_steps: Identify temporary housing outside the hills and keep insurance, medication, pet, school, and work records accessible.
precision_limitations: This is area-based LHMP context. Confirm fire-zone membership with the official CAL FIRE or local fire-zone layer.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex describes Fremont extending into the East Bay Hills toward Sunol; separate city rules designate wildland-urban-interface fire areas.

location_cue: Fremont Baylands and western flatlands
resident_meaning: Fremont's annex describes western Baylands, wetlands, lowlands, creeks, and the developed Bay Plain. These features make official flood and drainage checks especially important for western addresses.
before_actions: Review the FEMA address result, protect documents and medications above floor level, and identify a route away from flooded roads.
during_actions: Do not walk or drive through floodwater; use official road and evacuation information.
after_actions: Document damage before cleanup and avoid contaminated water or wet electrical systems.
recovery_steps: Plan for temporary housing, transportation disruption, insurance or landlord contact, and replacement of essential supplies.
precision_limitations: The broad Baylands/flatlands cue does not establish that a property is inside a FEMA flood zone.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex describes Baylands habitat zones, lowlands, the developed Bay Plain, and natural and man-made creeks.

location_cue: Fremont neighborhoods identified in the annex's social-vulnerability discussion
resident_meaning: The Fremont annex identifies concentrations of social vulnerability west of Fremont Boulevard in Centerville, Glenmoor, Sundale, and Irvington. This affects preparedness and recovery support needs, not the probability of ground shaking at an individual property.
before_actions: Plan communication, transportation, medication access, and neighbor support for an extended utility or road disruption.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on during shaking, then follow official safety information.
after_actions: Check neighbors who may need communication, mobility, medical, or transportation assistance when safe.
recovery_steps: Prepare for housing, language-access, transportation, school, work, and financial recovery needs.
resident_impacts: Unequal access to transportation, information, and recovery resources
precision_limitations: This is neighborhood-level vulnerability context and does not measure structural or parcel-level seismic risk.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex identifies social-vulnerability concentrations west of Fremont Boulevard in named neighborhoods.

location_cue: Hayward Fault corridor along the base of the hills
resident_meaning: Hayward's Local Resilience Plan states that the Hayward Fault traverses the city along the base of the hillside. A nearby fault-line result should be paired with planning for severe shaking, utility interruption, damaged roads, and building access.
before_actions: Secure heavy items, prepare for utility outages, and plan how household members will reunite if roads or communications fail.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on; stay away from windows and falling objects.
after_actions: Expect aftershocks and do not remain in a visibly damaged building.
recovery_steps: Identify temporary housing and keep medication, insurance, lease or mortgage, transportation, and emergency-contact information accessible.
precision_limitations: The LHMP corridor description is area context; fault proximity comes from the loaded official fault data, not this text rule.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan describes the Hayward Fault traversing the city along the base of the hillside.

location_cue: Eastern Hayward Hills
resident_meaning: Hayward's plan identifies the eastern Hayward Hills as especially vulnerable to wildfire because of heavier vegetation, while steep slopes also increase landslide concerns.
before_actions: Prepare for early evacuation, two routes, smoke protection, medication and pet supplies, and transportation backup.
during_actions: Leave when officials instruct; do not wait for visible flames if routes may become constrained.
after_actions: Wait for official re-entry and document smoke, evacuation, utility, and property impacts.
recovery_steps: Plan temporary housing and continuity for medications, pets, school, work, and transportation.
precision_limitations: Confirm fire-hazard-zone membership with the official CAL FIRE layer.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan identifies eastern Hayward Hills as more heavily vegetated and wildfire-vulnerable.

location_cue: South Hayward near Ward Creek and the Bay shoreline
resident_meaning: Hayward's plan identifies residential and industrial parts of South Hayward near the Don Edwards refuge and Ward Creek as exposed to flooding under higher-water scenarios.
before_actions: Check the FEMA address result, protect documents and medicines, and identify routes that avoid shoreline or creek flooding.
during_actions: Move away from rising water and never drive through floodwater.
after_actions: Document damage and avoid contaminated water, mold, and wet electrical systems.
recovery_steps: Prepare for temporary housing, transportation disruption, insurance or landlord contact, and prolonged drainage problems.
precision_limitations: This LHMP area cue supplements but does not replace FEMA flood-zone membership.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan identifies parts of South Hayward adjacent to the refuge and Ward Creek as flood-exposed.

location_cue: Alameda shoreline and low-lying island context
resident_meaning: Alameda's plan identifies shoreline flooding, rising groundwater, storm-drain demands, and transportation dependencies near Doolittle Drive and the Posey/Webster Tubes. FEMA flood status remains the address-level evidence.
before_actions: Review the FEMA result, protect documents and medications, and identify more than one route off or across the island.
during_actions: Avoid flooded streets and follow official transportation and evacuation instructions.
after_actions: Document water damage and check electrical, mold, contamination, and transportation conditions before returning to normal routines.
recovery_steps: Plan temporary housing, insurance or landlord contact, medicine access, school/work continuity, and alternatives if tube or shoreline routes are disrupted.
precision_limitations: This is area-based adaptation-plan context; it does not establish parcel flood depth or groundwater conditions.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan identifies shoreline adaptation work near Doolittle Drive and the Posey/Webster Tubes and discusses rising groundwater and storm drainage.
source_name: Alameda Climate Adaptation and Hazard Mitigation Plan

location_cue: Older building stock, nearby faults, and liquefaction-sensitive areas
resident_meaning: Alameda's plan identifies earthquake vulnerability from older buildings, nearby faults, liquefaction, and the need to restore services after shaking.
before_actions: Secure movable items, review retrofit or soft-story concerns where applicable, and prepare for utility and transportation disruption.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Check for injuries, fire, gas, structural damage, and unsafe utilities.
recovery_steps: Prepare housing, insurance, medication, transportation, school, and work continuity plans.
precision_limitations: Liquefaction and building vulnerability require official layers or property-specific evaluation.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan prioritizes earthquake property/life-safety mitigation, liquefaction exposure, and timely service restoration.
source_name: Alameda Climate Adaptation and Hazard Mitigation Plan

named_areas: Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone
location_cue: Oakland Hills
resident_meaning: Oakland's draft plan states that much of the Oakland Hills is designated Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone and receives annual vegetation-management inspections. Official CAL FIRE/local fire-zone data remains the address-level evidence.
before_actions: Prepare for early evacuation, keep alerts active, identify two routes, and plan for medications, pets, smoke, and transportation.
during_actions: Leave when officials instruct and avoid waiting for visible flames if roads may close.
after_actions: Wait for re-entry instructions and document evacuation, smoke, utility, and property impacts.
recovery_steps: Identify temporary housing outside the hills and keep insurance, lease or mortgage, medication, pet, school, and work records ready.
precision_limitations: The area rule is not a parcel fire-zone boundary; use the official fire layer for zone membership.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan describes annual inspections of roughly 25,000 Oakland Hills parcels in the VHFHSZ.

location_cue: Oakland shoreline, port, and airport areas
resident_meaning: Oakland's draft plan describes shoreline and port adaptation work, including a localized flood-protection project near Old Earhart Road and North Field. This context supplements FEMA address checks.
before_actions: Review the FEMA result, protect records and medicines, and plan routes that avoid shoreline or airport-area flooding.
during_actions: Avoid floodwater and follow official road, port, airport, and evacuation information.
after_actions: Document damage and verify utilities, contamination, access roads, and building safety.
recovery_steps: Plan for transportation, work, housing, insurance or landlord contact, and prolonged infrastructure disruption.
precision_limitations: This area context does not establish flood-zone membership or expected depth at a property.
source_excerpt_summary: The draft plan describes shoreline adaptation and the Old Earhart Road/North Field floodwall project.

location_cue: Berkeley Fire Zones 2 and 3
resident_meaning: Berkeley's LHMP identifies Fire Zone 3/Panoramic Hill as its greatest WUI vulnerability and Fire Zone 2 across the eastern hills, with access and egress constraints.
before_actions: Know two evacuation routes, prepare to leave early during extreme fire weather, and plan transportation, medications, pets, and smoke protection.
during_actions: Follow official evacuation instructions and do not wait for visible flames.
after_actions: Wait for official re-entry and document smoke, evacuation, utility, and property impacts.
recovery_steps: Prepare temporary housing and pet-friendly options outside the hills.
precision_limitations: Confirm the address's official Berkeley fire-zone designation before treating it as Zone 2 or 3.
source_excerpt_summary: The LHMP identifies Panoramic Hill as Fire Zone 3 and Berkeley's greatest WUI vulnerability because of access and egress constraints.

location_cue: West Berkeley and shoreline liquefaction areas
resident_meaning: Berkeley's LHMP identifies the Marina, Eastshore State Park, Aquatic Park, creek corridors, and western bands as areas with liquefaction-susceptible conditions.
before_actions: Prepare for severe shaking, utility interruption, damaged roads, and possible building displacement.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Expect aftershocks and avoid damaged buildings, roads, and utility hazards.
recovery_steps: Plan temporary housing, medication access, transportation alternatives, and insurance or landlord documentation.
precision_limitations: This LHMP named-area match is not an official point-in-polygon liquefaction result.
source_excerpt_summary: The LHMP lists the Marina, Eastshore, Aquatic Park, creek corridors, and west Berkeley bands in its liquefaction discussion.

location_cue: San Leandro bayside neighborhoods identified for liquefaction concern
resident_meaning: San Leandro's Environmental Hazards Element identifies high liquefaction concern in Marina Faire/Mulford Gardens and Washington Manor/Bonaire during a large Hayward Fault earthquake.
before_actions: Prepare for shaking, road and utility damage, and temporary displacement.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Avoid damaged buildings and utility hazards and follow inspection guidance.
recovery_steps: Keep housing, insurance or lease, medication, transportation, and family continuity plans ready.
precision_limitations: This is named-neighborhood source context, not an official address-level liquefaction overlay.
source_excerpt_summary: The element names Marina Faire/Mulford Gardens and Washington Manor/Bonaire as high liquefaction-risk neighborhoods.

location_cue: Codornices Creek and Albany shoreline areas
resident_meaning: Albany's draft update identifies flooding around Codornices Creek and shoreline locations including Albany Bulb and Albany Beach, with additional 500-year context south of Buchanan Street.
before_actions: Review the FEMA address result, protect records, and identify routes away from creek or shoreline flooding.
during_actions: Avoid floodwater and follow official road information.
after_actions: Document damage and avoid contaminated water and wet electrical systems.
recovery_steps: Prepare insurance or landlord, temporary housing, and transportation plans.
precision_limitations: Use FEMA mapping for address-level flood-zone status.
source_excerpt_summary: The draft identifies Codornices Creek, Albany Bulb, Albany Beach, and areas south of Buchanan Street in its FEMA flood discussion.

location_cue: Citywide shaking with liquefaction concern across part of Emeryville
resident_meaning: Emeryville's draft plan states that the entire city is subject to hazardous shaking and roughly half the city is subject to liquefaction in a major regional earthquake.
before_actions: Secure heavy items and prepare for utility, transportation, and building-access disruption.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Check for fire, gas, structural, and utility hazards and expect aftershocks.
recovery_steps: Prepare temporary housing, insurance or lease, medication, and transportation plans.
precision_limitations: The plan's citywide statement does not identify whether a specific address is inside a liquefaction zone.
source_excerpt_summary: The draft states that the entire city faces shaking and about half faces liquefaction.

location_cue: Newark shoreline and fill areas
resident_meaning: Newark's annex explains that liquefaction conditions are most common along the Bay shoreline, near creeks and waterways, dry creek beds, and areas of man-made fill.
before_actions: Prepare for shaking, road and pipe damage, utility interruption, and temporary displacement.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Avoid damaged roads, buildings, and utility lines and expect aftershocks.
recovery_steps: Plan for housing, transportation, sanitation, water, medication, and insurance needs.
precision_limitations: Official CGS or local liquefaction mapping is required for address-level status.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex explains shoreline, creek, saturated-soil, and fill conditions associated with liquefaction.

location_cue: Union City western baylands and flood-control channels
resident_meaning: Union City's annex describes western shoreline wetlands and flood-control channels as major open-space systems. FEMA and local flood-control mapping remain necessary for an address result.
before_actions: Review FEMA results, protect records and medicines, and plan routes away from flooded channels or low-lying roads.
during_actions: Avoid floodwater and follow official road and evacuation instructions.
after_actions: Document damage and verify utilities and sanitation before returning.
recovery_steps: Plan for housing, transportation, water, wastewater, insurance or landlord, and school/work continuity.
precision_limitations: This is area context, not a flood-zone or expected-depth determination.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex describes western wetlands, the eastern Hillside Area, and flood-control channels as major open-space systems.

location_cue: West and Downtown Dublin development areas
resident_meaning: The Dublin annex identifies portions of downtown projects within the Alquist-Priolo fault zone and medium liquefaction zone, while some western projects are within high wildfire severity areas.
before_actions: Prepare for shaking, utility disruption, and transportation impacts; verify official fault, liquefaction, flood, and fire maps for the address.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on during shaking.
after_actions: Avoid damaged buildings and utilities and expect transportation delays.
recovery_steps: Prepare housing, transportation, medication, insurance, and work continuity plans.
precision_limitations: The annex describes project areas; official geospatial layers are required before applying those zones to an individual address.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex describes West and Downtown Dublin projects intersecting wildfire, fault, liquefaction, and FEMA flood contexts.

location_cue: Pleasanton ridges and Southeast Hills
resident_meaning: The Pleasanton annex describes the Southeast Hills and the Pleasanton/Main Ridges as steep hillside terrain bordering developed areas, supporting area-based wildfire and evacuation planning.
before_actions: Turn on alerts, identify evacuation routes, and prepare for smoke, pets, medications, and transportation.
during_actions: Follow official evacuation instructions and leave early if routes may become constrained.
after_actions: Wait for official re-entry and document evacuation, smoke, utility, and property impacts.
recovery_steps: Plan temporary housing and continuity for medications, pets, school, work, and transportation.
precision_limitations: Confirm fire-zone membership with official CAL FIRE or local mapping.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex describes the Southeast Hills and seismically active Pleasanton and Main Ridges.

location_cue: Citywide wildfire and hillside context
resident_meaning: Piedmont's 2019 plan ranks wildfire as extensive, highly likely, and potentially catastrophic, while also identifying landslide and localized stormwater concerns.
before_actions: Turn on alerts, prepare evacuation routes, and plan for smoke, medications, pets, and transportation.
during_actions: Follow official evacuation instructions.
after_actions: Wait for re-entry guidance and document smoke, evacuation, and property impacts.
recovery_steps: Prepare temporary housing, insurance, medication, pet, school, and work continuity plans.
precision_limitations: This record is citywide plan context; official fire-zone mapping is required for address status.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan's risk table ranks wildfire and other city hazards by extent, likelihood, and impact.

named_areas: Alameda County Water District service area
location_cue: Tri-City water and wastewater service dependencies
resident_meaning: The water and sanitary district annexes identify earthquake and liquefaction as important threats to water and wastewater infrastructure serving Fremont, Newark, and Union City.
before_actions: Store water and plan how to manage sanitation, medication, and powered-device needs during an extended service interruption.
during_actions: Follow official boil-water, conservation, and sanitation instructions.
after_actions: Do not assume tap water or wastewater service is safe until the responsible district confirms service status.
recovery_steps: Plan for replacement water, sanitation supplies, medical hygiene, temporary housing, and business or school interruption.
precision_limitations: This is utility service-area recovery context, not a prediction that service will fail at a specific address.
source_excerpt_summary: Both district annexes describe earthquake and liquefaction threats to their Tri-City service territories.
source_name: 2024 Tri-City LHMP, Water and Sanitary District Annexes

location_cue: Livermore earthquake and transportation-crossing context
resident_meaning: The Livermore annex records damage from the 1980 Greenville Fault earthquake and identifies earthquake evaluation and backup needs at road and rail undercrossings. Residents should prepare for transportation and utility disruption even when an address is not near a mapped fault trace.
before_actions: Secure heavy items and plan multiple transportation, communication, and household reunification options.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Expect aftershocks and check official road, rail, utility, and building-safety information.
recovery_steps: Prepare temporary housing, medication, transportation, insurance, school, and work continuity plans.
precision_limitations: This is citywide infrastructure context; official fault and ground-failure layers are required for address-specific claims.
source_excerpt_summary: The annex records the 1980 Greenville Fault earthquake and discusses earthquake vulnerability and backup pumps at named transportation crossings.

location_cue: Unincorporated Alameda County community context
resident_meaning: The county plan separately identifies unincorporated communities and includes official fire-hazard-severity and wildland-urban-interface maps. StayReady should use the CAL FIRE layer for an address result and county plan context for preparedness and recovery.
before_actions: Turn on county alerts, prepare evacuation and smoke plans, and confirm official fire-zone information for the address.
during_actions: Follow Alameda County evacuation and emergency instructions.
after_actions: Wait for official re-entry and document smoke, evacuation, utility, and property impacts.
recovery_steps: Prepare housing, transportation, medication, pet, insurance, school, and work continuity plans.
precision_limitations: The county plan map inventory does not itself establish address-level fire-zone membership in StayReady.
source_excerpt_summary: The plan identifies unincorporated communities and includes fire-hazard-severity and WUI maps in its appendix.
source_name: Alameda County 2021 Local Hazard Mitigation Plan

location_cue: Tri-City regional earthquake and lifeline context
resident_meaning: The Tri-City Base Plan identifies earthquakes as a significant planning-area hazard and makes resilient potable-water and wastewater systems an explicit mitigation goal.
before_actions: Store water and prepare for transportation, communication, power, water, and wastewater interruption after strong shaking.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Check official water, sanitation, road, utility, and building-safety information.
recovery_steps: Plan for replacement water, sanitation, medication, temporary housing, transportation, school, and work continuity.
precision_limitations: This is multi-jurisdictional recovery context and does not describe structural risk at a specific property.
source_excerpt_summary: The Base Plan identifies earthquake among significant hazards and prioritizes resilient potable-water and wastewater systems.

location_cue: Tri-Valley regional earthquake and lifeline context
resident_meaning: Tri-Valley Volume 1 assesses earthquake exposure across people, buildings, transportation, bridges, roads, sewers, water systems, electricity, and communications. City annexes provide the more specific local priorities.
before_actions: Prepare for several days of transportation, power, water, sanitation, and communication disruption after a regional earthquake.
during_actions: Drop, cover, and hold on.
after_actions: Check official road, transit, water, utility, and building-safety information.
recovery_steps: Plan for housing, medication, transportation, water, sanitation, school, work, and financial continuity.
precision_limitations: This is regional lifeline context; city annexes and official GIS checks determine more local evidence.
source_excerpt_summary: Volume 1 contains the regional earthquake assessment and defines exposed assets to include transportation, water, sewer, power, and communications.
